title: Finite Value Iteration

solver_reuse.target: finite value-iteration bad Bellman-backup QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact finite replay computes the second-iteration Bellman backup Q2(s1, a) = 5/2 from the committed MDP table, while a malformed row claims 2, isolating a compact scalar Farkas conflict beneath the value-iteration trace.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional dynamic-programming rows only when they add distinct policy-iteration, undiscounted/average-reward, stochastic-approximation/Q-learning, larger-horizon, or numerical-honesty pressure; keep the Banach fixed-point theorem, value-iteration convergence in general, greedy-policy optimality theorems, and floating-point dynamic-programming claims in theorem or numerical-honesty lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed finite MDP table, the discount factor, every iteration's Bellman backups, greedy values and policies, the exact fixed point, and the sup-norm contraction steps as deterministic exact-rational replay obligations.
Route the false Bellman-backup claim through a source-linked QF_LRA artifact and checked Farkas evidence after exact replay computes the conflicting value equation.
Expose the pack through probability, dynamics, optimization, value-iteration, exact-vs-floating, and learner-query surfaces.

Keep the Banach fixed-point theorem, value-iteration and policy-iteration convergence in general, greedy-policy optimality theorems, infinite-horizon and continuous MDP theory, stochastic approximation, and floating-point dynamic programming marked theorem or numerical-honesty horizon.
